title: Performance Optimization (Quick Wins)
description: Implement low-risk, high-impact performance optimizations from the Future Features Roadmap: Set-based lookups, NameIndex utilization, and batch operation Maps

sprints.title: Benchmark Foundation
sprints.focus: Establish baseline performance metrics before any optimizations

sprints.title: Set-Based Lookup Optimization
sprints.focus: Replace array.includes() with Set.has() for O(1) bulk delete lookups

sprints.title: NameIndex Utilization
sprints.focus: Use existing O(1) NameIndex instead of O(n) find() calls for entity existence checks where applicable

sprints.title: Batch Operation Optimization
sprints.focus: Build lookup Maps for batch updates and finalize with version bump

dependencyGraph.mermaid:
graph TD
    S1[Sprint 1: Benchmark Foundation] --> S2[Sprint 2: Set-Based Lookups]
    S1 --> S3[Sprint 3: NameIndex Utilization]
    S2 --> S4[Sprint 4: Batch Operations & Release]
    S3 --> S4

parallelExecutionGroups.description: Baseline - must complete first to measure improvements
Can run in parallel after Sprint 1

riskAssessment.mitigation: Verify index updates on mutation paths

haiku.description: All tasks are straightforward optimizations suitable for Haiku

technicalNotes: Set.has() is O(1) vs array.includes() which is O(n)
NameIndex already exists in GraphStorage.ts at line ~609
Map lookup is O(1) vs array.find() which is O(n)
All optimizations are internal - no API changes
notes: These are 'quick wins' - low effort, high impact optimizations that require minimal code changes. Sprint 1 establishes baselines so we can measure actual improvements. Sprints 2 and 3 can run in parallel. All changes are internal to EntityManager.ts with no API changes.
